findings.message: New `redis-exporter` service exposes Redis metrics on :9121, but `infra/observability/prometheus/prometheus.yml` has no scrape_config targeting `redis-exporter:9121` (it only scrapes prometheus, otel-collector, and postgres-exporter). The exporter is added to the prod-bound `observability.yml` overlay, so Redis — a prod runtime data store defined in `base.yml:40` — produces zero collected metrics. An exporter with no scrape job is dead weight: the observability never actually reaches Prometheus.
findings.suggestion:
Add a scrape job to `prometheus.yml`:
  - job_name: redis
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["redis-exporter:9121"]

findings.message: The new `redis-exporter` wires up a Redis observability surface, but there is no corresponding alert rule file under `infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/` (existing files cover only alertmanager, otel-collector, postgres, prometheus, slo). Redis is a prod runtime dependency, so adding the exporter without any alerts (e.g. redis down, memory/maxmemory pressure, rejected connections, replication lag) leaves the surface unmonitored despite now being instrumented.
findings.suggestion: Add `infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml` with at least a RedisDown alert (`redis_up == 0`) routed to an existing Alertmanager receiver, plus memory/eviction alerts.

findings.message: No Grafana dashboard accompanies the new `redis-exporter`. Existing dashboards in `infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/` cover otel-collector, postgres, prometheus, and slo — there is no redis.json. The pattern in this repo is exporter + scrape job + alert rule + dashboard per surface (cf. postgres-exporter, which has postgres_alerts.yml and postgres.json); the Redis surface is missing the dashboard half of that contract.
findings.suggestion: Add `infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/redis.json` with panels for the key `redis_exporter` metrics (uptime, memory, connected clients, ops/sec, evictions) so the new metrics are visualized, mirroring the postgres dashboard.
